title: The Irishman
logline: An aging hitman recounts his decades-long involvement with the Bufalino crime family and his role in the disappearance of his friend Jimmy Hoffa.
antagonistOrConflict: The inevitable consequences of a life in organized crime and the moral weight of betraying friendship for loyalty to the mob
themes: The corruption of the American Dream

actBreaks.description: Frank meets Jimmy Hoffa for the first time, launching their friendship and Frank's deeper involvement in Teamster affairs.

validation.ruling: Scene #125 shows a routine envelope exchange which doesn't create irreversible change. Scene #124 shows Jimmy Hoffa's first confrontation with Bobby Kennedy at the McClellan Committee hearing, establishing the Kennedy-Hoffa conflict that will drive the entire second act and fundamentally escalate the stakes for all characters.

actBreaks.description: Frank kills Jimmy Hoffa in the Pontiac house, completing his ultimate betrayal and marking the end of their relationship.

validation.ruling: Scene #238 is the climax itself, not an act break. Scene #235 shows Jimmy's angry confrontation at the Red Fox parking lot where he realizes he's been betrayed, marking the point of no return where the final deadly confrontation becomes inevitable and Frank can no longer avoid choosing between his loyalties.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a sophisticated circular structure that begins and ends with an elderly Frank being interviewed about a house, creating a frame narrative that emphasizes the weight of memory and consequence. The story unfolds through extensive flashbacks that spiral through decades, moving between Frank's present-day recollections and his past experiences. Rather than following a traditional three-act structure, it operates more like a memoir, with the Hoffa relationship serving as the central spine around which all other criminal activities revolve. The structure mirrors the way memory works - non-linear, associative, and haunted by pivotal moments.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately methodical and contemplative, reflecting an old man's measured recollection of his life. Long stretches focus on relationship-building and the mundane details of criminal enterprise, punctuated by sudden moments of violence. The script takes its time establishing Frank's world before introducing Hoffa around scene 125, then accelerates through their friendship and business relationship before slowing again for the inevitable, tragic conclusion. The final act becomes increasingly sparse and melancholic as Frank faces the isolation that comes with outliving everyone he knew.

incitingIncident.description: A younger Russell sits at Villa D'Roma with one of Frank's stolen steaks on his plate.

validation.ruling: Scene #30 shows Frank's truck breaking down, which is merely a mechanical problem that doesn't shatter his ordinary world or establish main conflict. Scene #49 at Villa D'Roma shows Frank meeting Russell Bufalino for the first time, which fundamentally changes his trajectory from small-time truck driver to connected mob associate, establishing the central relationship and criminal world that drives the entire story.

midpoint.description: The prison bus pulls through the gates of Lewisburg Federal Penitentiary.

validation.ruling: Scene #160 merely shows a prison bus arriving at Lewisburg, which is transitional setup rather than a transformative moment. Scene #168 shows Frank's extended conversation with Jimmy Hoffa in prison where they bond deeply and Frank becomes Jimmy's trusted ally, marking Frank's shift from reactive follower to proactive player in the power struggle between the Teamsters and the mob.

climax.description: Jimmy immediately senses danger upon entering the quiet house with no voices and poorly cut linoleum underfoot, while Frank confirms in voiceover that Jimmy knew what it was.

validation.ruling: Scene #238 is the ultimate confrontation where Frank shoots Jimmy Hoffa in the house in Pontiac, resolving the central conflict between loyalty to Russell versus loyalty to Jimmy, demonstrating Frank's completed character arc as he chooses the mob family over his friend.

scenes.heading: INT. MOTEL - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank sits disturbed in a motel room being filmed, reluctantly agreeing to repeat on camera that he stands by what he previously told an interviewer.

scenes.heading: INT. MOTEL ROOM - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank sits alone in pajamas watching video playback of an empty house being shown by a realtor.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: A friend gives another friend a tip on a race or football game on a street corner.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANOTHER STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: A friend gets out of a car to let another friend take the front passenger seat.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: A friend cooks eggs and sausages for another friend, then three men are shot in the back of their heads in quick succession, with Giancana falling to the floor.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - MORNING - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank packs for a wedding trip to Detroit, putting on his watch and gold ring while explaining the significance of Bill Bufalino's daughter's wedding.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRANK’S HOUSE - MORNING - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank loads luggage into his Cadillac trunk, preparing for a road trip with Russell who wants to handle business along the way.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - MORNING - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank draws a route on a AAA map while his wife Irene talks to Carrie about what to wear to the wedding.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUFALINO’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank and Russell Bufalino load luggage while their wives smoke cigarettes on the sidewalk before departing for Detroit.

scenes.heading: EXT. OCEAN - DAY
scenes.summary: A pack of cigarettes floats on ocean water as a cabin cruiser motors away in the distance.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S CADILLAC - PHILADELPHIA - DAY - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank drives through Philadelphia with Russell and their wives, discussing Russell's irritation about smoking and their losses in Cuba.

scenes.heading: EXT. PHILADELPHIA - DAY - 1975
scenes.summary: The women smoke outside the car while Russell studies the map and Frank watches children play soccer at a Catholic school.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANZIO, ITALY - DAWN - 1943
scenes.summary: American soldiers dig frantically in the sand at Anzio beach as mortars explode around them, with soldiers falling dead on both sides of Frank.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANZIO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank and other soldiers take shelter in a deep, mine-like foxhole reinforced with planks as rain causes dangerous mud cave-ins.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANZIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank urinates into a helmet in his foxhole and carefully disposes of it, explaining the deadly constraints of daylight movement.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANZIO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank listens from his foxhole as the German artillery piece called the Anzio Express moves on railroad tracks and shells hit other soldiers.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANZIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank observes a long row of dead soldiers in a trench while fresh recruits arrive, with one new replacement immediately getting his head shot off.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANZIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank and veteran soldiers play cards while deliberately ignoring new recruits who sit apart from them.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANZIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank eats from a can in the trench when the German position gets hit from behind, allowing him to cautiously peer over the foxhole.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANZIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank and other survivors execute German prisoners, with Frank shooting Germans begging for mercy and forcing one to dig his own grave.
scenes.turningPointNote: Frank's capacity for cold-blooded killing is established, setting up his future as a mob enforcer

scenes.heading: EXT. ITALY - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank marches through an Italian town and notices locals switching from German flags to American flags.

scenes.heading: INT. BOARDING HOUSE - PHILLY - NIGHT - 1946
scenes.summary: Frank lies on a bed in a cheap boarding house room, staring at the ceiling as his Army money runs out.

scenes.heading: INT. BLOOD BANK - PHILLY - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank sells his blood at a blood bank for ten dollars a pint.

scenes.heading: INT. WAGNER’S DANCE HALL - NIGHT - 1947
scenes.summary: Frank works as a bouncer throwing out fighters at a dance hall and notices a shy girl sitting along the wall.

scenes.heading: INT. MOTHER OF SORROWS CHURCH - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank marries the shy girl in a church while her father looks unhappy about the union.

scenes.heading: INT. MARY’S PARENTS’ HOUSE - PHILLY - DAY - 1948
scenes.summary: Frank eats dinner in tense silence with Mary's disapproving father while an infant sits in a highchair.

scenes.heading: EXT. SWIFT’S MEAT COMPANY - PHILLY - DAY - 1949
scenes.summary: Frank works loading meat at Swift's company and asks driver Joey about becoming a driver himself.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. FRANK’S CADILLAC / HIGHWAY - DAY - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank drives his Cadillac with Russell and their wives on Highway 276, with Irene asking to stop soon.

scenes.heading: EXT. GAS STATION - DAY - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank fills the gas tank while the women smoke and Russell buys gum, with Russell and Frank regarding their surroundings meaningfully.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. HIGHWAY 476 - DAY - 1950’S
scenes.summary: Frank drives a truck along the same Highway 476 twenty-five years earlier when the engine starts making problematic noises.
scenes.turningPointNote: The truck breakdown that leads to Frank meeting Russell Bufalino, the pivotal relationship of his life

scenes.heading: EXT. GAS STATION - DAY - 1950’S
scenes.summary: Russell appears with tools to help Frank fix his truck's carburetor problem, successfully repairing the engine and shaking Frank's hand.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTH PHILLY - NIGHT - 1950’S
scenes.summary: The exterior of The Friendly Lounge is shown according to its sign.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FRIENDLY LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank hangs out with Italian truck drivers while Joey introduces him to Skinny Razor, who owns the lounge and runs small-time gambling operations.

scenes.heading: INT. BUTCHER SHOP - DAY
scenes.summary: A younger Skinny works at a butcher shop, using a straight razor to cut the throats of chickens selected by Italian customers.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FRIENDLY LOUNGE - CONTINUED
scenes.summary: Frank offers to deliver steak to Skinny, who shows interest in the proposition.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEAT COMPANY - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank signs for a truck load of meat hindquarters as the yard manager places an aluminum seal on the lock.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOOD FAIR MARKET - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank delivers meat to a Food Fair Market where the manager breaks the seal, then explains his winter theft scheme involving lazy yard managers.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FRIENDLY LOUNGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Skinny's men carry stolen hindquarters into the Friendly Lounge while Skinny pays Frank cash and Frank puts a new seal on his truck.

scenes.heading: INT. MEAT COMPANY - DAY
scenes.summary: The yard manager warms his hands over a heater, too lazy to go outside and properly check Frank's truck in the cold.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FRIENDLY LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Skinny and his mob friends enjoy an inexpensive steak dinner courtesy of Frank's stolen meat operation.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOOD FAIR MARKET - DAY
scenes.summary: The store manager breaks Frank's truck seal to reveal a completely empty truck, with Frank pretending to be mystified about the missing meat.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOCAL 107 - PHILADELPHIA - DAY
scenes.summary: The exterior of Teamsters Local 107 is shown with its familiar union logo on the door.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCAL 107 - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank meets with young Teamster lawyer Bill Bufalino, who questions him about his work record and asks directly if he stole the meat.

scenes.heading: INT. COURTROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Bill Bufalino successfully defends Frank in court, arguing that Frank couldn't name conspirators because they don't exist, with the judge throwing out the case.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FRIENDLY LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank receives a hero's welcome at the Friendly Lounge and is introduced to higher mob figure Angelo Bruno, who praises him for not ratting anyone out.
scenes.turningPointNote: Frank's loyalty under pressure earns him respect from higher-level mob figures, advancing his criminal career

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLA D’ROMA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The exterior of Villa D'Roma restaurant is shown, where Frank will be properly introduced to Russell Bufalino.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S CADILLAC - MOVING - DAY - 1975
scenes.summary: Russell naps in the passenger seat while Frank drives, with Carrie asking Frank to stop after an hour of driving.

scenes.heading: EXT. RURAL PENNSYLVANIA - DAY - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank pulls over outside Wilkes-Barre for the women to smoke while Russell mentions having business to do there before napping again.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA D’ROMA - NIGHT - 1950’S
scenes.summary: A younger Russell sits at Villa D'Roma with one of Frank's stolen steaks on his plate.

scenes.heading: INT. CURTAIN SHOP - PITTSTON, PA. - DAY - 1950’S
scenes.summary: A succession of men meet with Russell in the back of his curtain shop in Pittston, seeking his permission for bribes, promotions, and eliminations.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA D’ROMA - NIGHT - 1950’S - CONTINUED
scenes.summary: Russell compliments Frank for his actions on the highway and shares prosciutto bread with him, pleased when Frank responds in Italian and reveals he learned it during the war in Italy.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR - MOVING - DAY - 1999
scenes.summary: Frank rides as a passenger in a car traveling north to south on the highway, passing a motel.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOTEL - WILKES-BARRE - DAY - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank pulls his Cadillac off the highway at the same motel, traveling in the opposite direction.

scenes.heading: INT. MOTEL LOBBY - WILKES-BARRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank and Russell check into the motel while their wives smoke outside, as Frank narrates that he quit his job but kept his union card and started doing business for Skinny's crew.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSE - PHILLY - DAY - 1950’S
scenes.summary: Frank sits in an idling car as Skinny hurries out in a robe to hand him a paper bag, instructing him to show it but not use it.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S CAR - LATER - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank takes a gun from the bag, gets out of his car, and intercepts a terrified man on the sidewalk who goes with him without argument.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S CAR - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank drives with the man who has urinated in his pants from fear, rolling down the window due to the smell.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FRIENDLY LOUNGE - LATER - DAY
scenes.summary: Skinny confronts the wet-pants debtor who promises to pay tomorrow, then dismisses him to take a bus home, while Frank complains about the man peeing in his car.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA D’ROMA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank's family dines with Russell and Carrie at Villa d'Roma, where Russell tries to show Peggy how to eat prosciutto bread dipped in wine, but she refuses and appears uncomfortable around him.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Frank comes home to find Peggy sulking because a grocer slapped her for eating a grape, and despite Mary's dismissal of the incident, Frank takes Peggy with him to confront the grocer.

scenes.heading: EXT. GROCERY STORE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Frank brutally crushes the grocer's hand on the curb with his foot after the man claims to know judo, leaving Peggy stunned and staring at the writhing man.
scenes.turningPointNote: Frank's violent retaliation in front of his daughter marks a turning point in their relationship and reveals his true nature to Peggy

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The family eats dinner in silence as Peggy steals fearful glances at her father, now afraid of him after witnessing his violence.

scenes.heading: INT. MOTHER OF SORROWS CHURCH - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank's third daughter is baptized at church with family and associates like Skinny present, as Frank narrates about Whispers DiTullio approaching him about needing ten grand.

scenes.heading: INT. BOCCE CLUB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Whispers whispers to Frank at the Bocce Club about pushing too much money to a laundry business and being concerned about only getting the interest payments.

scenes.heading: INT. LINEN SUPPLY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank observes the linen supply house with many idle machines, narrating that it should normally be a profitable business.

scenes.heading: INT. BOCCE CLUB - CONTINUED
scenes.summary: Whispers explains that another laundry in Delaware is siphoning off their business.

scenes.heading: INT. CADILLAC LINEN SERVICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank observes the busy competing Cadillac Linen Service while Whispers expresses being more than a little concerned about his investment.

scenes.heading: INT. BOCCE CLUB - CONTINUED
scenes.summary: Whispers admits he's more than a little concerned about the situation.

scenes.heading: EXT. CADILLAC LINEN SERVICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank watches workers loading trucks at Cadillac Linen Service while narrating that Whispers doesn't want him to just show a gun for ten grand.

scenes.heading: INT. BOCCE CLUB - CONTINUED
scenes.summary: Whispers passes Frank an envelope and explicitly asks him to bomb or burn down the Cadillac Linen Service, but Frank discovers it contains only two thousand dollars instead of the promised ten grand.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank gives most of the money to Mary, claiming he won it on a bet, while Peggy knows he's lying and cares that he is.

scenes.heading: EXT. CADILLAC LINEN SERVICE - DELAWARE - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank conducts surveillance of the Cadillac Linen Service building during the day, noting the burglar alarm system.

scenes.heading: EXT. CADILLAC LINEN SERVICE - DELAWARE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank continues his surveillance at night, examining the building and peering through windows at the machinery inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. GAS STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank fills gas cans at a station and loads them in his trunk alongside dynamite, preparing for the bombing.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank puts on dark clothes and heads for the door to carry out the bombing.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRANK’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: As Frank exits his house ready to bomb the laundry, Skinny appears by his car and tells him Angelo wants to see him.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA D’ROMA - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank is brought to an empty Villa d'Roma where Angelo and Russell confront him about his Delaware activities, and Frank admits he's planning to blow up a laundry service for Whispers.
scenes.turningPointNote: Frank's confession to the mob bosses about the unauthorized bombing job creates a life-threatening crisis that will determine his fate

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank shoots Whispers in the head at close range on the street as Whispers approaches him like a friend.
scenes.turningPointNote: Frank commits his first murder on orders from the mob bosses, crossing a line that fundamentally changes his role in the organization

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Frank sits at his kitchen table staring at the newspaper photo of Whispers' dead body, realizing that could have been him if not for Russell saving his life.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. CADILLAC - WILKES BARRE - DAY - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank and Russell drive through Wilkes-Barre as Russell goes into a jewelry store to collect money, while Frank narrates that everyone started treating him differently after the murder.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FRIENDLY LOUNGE - DAY - 1950’S
scenes.summary: Frank and Skinny sit at the bar as Frank narrates that Skinny no longer lets him pay for drinks.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FRIENDLY LOUNGE - NIGHT - 1950’S
scenes.summary: Frank and Angelo Bruno sit together as Frank narrates that Angelo won't let him pay for dinner.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA D’ROMA - DAY - 1950’S
scenes.summary: Frank sits with Russell and two Jewish mobsters at Villa d'Roma, where everyone treats him with new respect, including the flirtatious waitress, all because of the Whispers killing.

scenes.heading: EXT. PHILADELPHIA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank walks down a rain-slicked Philadelphia street with the Villa d'Roma waitress, narrating that he's now part of the downtown culture and that this is when he left his wife.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FRIENDLY LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank sits alone at The Friendly Lounge reviewing a list of names and numbers, crossing out some and circling others as he manages his own loan sharking business.

scenes.heading: INT. THE YESTERYEAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank meets with a crying debtor at The Yesteryear who explains his mother's funeral expenses prevented him from repaying the loan.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FRIENDLY LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Skinny reveals to Frank that the debtor's mother died ten years ago, exposing the lie about recent funeral expenses.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE YESTERYEAR BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank parks outside The Yesteryear bar and heads for the entrance.

scenes.heading: INT. THE YESTERYEAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Frank drags the lying debtor from his booth and beats him bloody on the floor, then argues with Harry the Hunchback about the mess and the man's debts to both of them.

scenes.heading: EXT. WAREHOUSE DISTRICT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Boxes of hijacked jewelry are transferred between cars in a warehouse district as Frank narrates about another debtor who failed to pay for stolen goods.

scenes.heading: INT. THE FRIENDLY LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank sits with his second wife Irene and Angelo Bruno's wife while Angelo privately tells Frank that Russell needs a favor involving delivering a message beyond just showing a gun.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A man hands Frank a paper bag containing a new gun through his car window as Frank narrates about using fresh, unfired weapons for actual killings.

scenes.heading: INT. THE “JEWELER’S” HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The jeweler wakes to investigate a noise, takes his gun, and follows the sound to his basement where a washing machine is running, as Frank narrates about eliminating potential rats.

scenes.heading: INT. MOTEL LOBBY - MORNING - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank and Russell check out of the Wilkes-Barre motel as Russell insists on paying, while Frank narrates that Russell never had children.

scenes.heading: INT. BOWLING ALLEY - DAY - 1950’S
scenes.summary: Russell and Carrie bowl with Frank, his second wife Irene, and his four daughters, with Russell helping the girls while Peggy keeps her distance from him.

scenes.heading: INT. BOWLING ALLEY - LATER
scenes.summary: Frank and Russell drink beers while watching their families bowl, discussing Frank's desire for steadier work and Russell suggesting union organizing.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLA D’ROMA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank hurries through a thunderstorm from his car to Villa d'Roma.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA D’ROMA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Russell hands Frank the phone to speak with Jimmy Hoffa, who introduces himself as Frank realizes he's talking to one of the most famous Americans of the era.
scenes.turningPointNote: Frank's introduction to Jimmy Hoffa marks his entry into the highest levels of organized crime and union corruption

scenes.heading: EXT. CHICAGO - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank arrives in Chicago and meets Joey Glimco at Local 777, where he'll be working in Public Relations for Hoffa's organization.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Frank and the other men enter a closed bathhouse where food is laid out on long tables for a private union meeting conducted in Turkish bathrobes.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHICAGO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank hot-wires a rebel cabbie's car from underneath the dashboard.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKE MICHIGAN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank and other Local 777 guys push stolen cabs into Lake Michigan while cops watch and receive payoff envelopes.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHICAGO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joey and Frank carry boxes of dynamite from a car trunk to a cab garage's back door.

scenes.heading: INT. CAB GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Taxi cabs in the silent garage suddenly start exploding.

scenes.heading: INT. GROCERY STORE - CHICAGO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joey Glimco shops for watermelons while warning Frank about Jimmy's punctuality expectations.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL SUITE - CHICAGO - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joey cuts a hole in a watermelon and pours a quart of rum into it while Frank checks his watch.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL SUITE - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jimmy Hoffa meets with Joey and Frank, studying Frank while Joey eats the rum-laced watermelon and suggests Frank stay in Chicago.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jimmy begins recruiting Frank for more serious work

scenes.heading: EXT. CHICAGO - DAY
scenes.summary: Jimmy asks Frank to help him 'straighten out a couple matters' by showing up while everything else is taken care of.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRANK’S HOUSE - PHILLY - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank returns home after flying to Puerto Rico, Detroit, and Chicago to take care of matters for Jimmy in one day.

scenes.heading: EXT. MINIATURE GOLF COURSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jimmy plays miniature golf with Frank's family, bonding with daughter Peggy while Jo Hoffa takes their picture together.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Peggy reads an essay about Jimmy Hoffa and the Teamsters Pension Fund to her class on Career Day with Frank present.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Irene brings out Peggy's birthday cake while Frank explains how Jimmy loans Pension Fund money to major mob bosses.

scenes.heading: INT. CURTAIN SHOP - PITTSTON, PA. - DAY
scenes.summary: Russell discusses fabric with someone in his legitimate curtain shop business.

scenes.heading: EXT. ATLANTIC CITY - DAY
scenes.summary: A man examines a vacant building site in Atlantic City.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: The man meets with a Teamster official wearing a union pin in an office.

scenes.heading: INT. CURTAIN SHOP - PITTSTON, PA - DAY
scenes.summary: The loan-seeking man speaks with Russell in the curtain shop about getting financing.

scenes.heading: INT. BANK - DAY
scenes.summary: Money is deposited into safety deposit boxes.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAS VEGAS - DAY
scenes.summary: A casino counting machine processes money in Las Vegas while building cranes dot the desert landscape.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAUTY PARLOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Jo Hoffa sits under a hair dryer in a beauty parlor among other women.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHICAGO - DAY
scenes.summary: Sam 'Momo' Giancana sits at a sidewalk table sipping espresso in Chicago.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAVEYARD - CHICAGO - DAY
scenes.summary: Men walk through a Chicago graveyard writing down names from tombstones.

scenes.heading: INT. POLLING PLACE - CHICAGO - DAY
scenes.summary: One of the men from the graveyard signs a dead person's name on a voting register at a polling place.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Jimmy Hoffa watches Kennedy's inauguration on television and declares his distrust of millionaire kids.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE CHAMBERS - DAY
scenes.summary: Jimmy Hoffa arrives on time for the McClellan Committee hearing but Bobby Kennedy is fifteen minutes late, then Hoffa is sworn in.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Kennedy-Hoffa conflict begins in earnest

scenes.heading: INT. NIGHTCLUB - KINGSTON - DAY - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank sits at a closed nightclub bar while Russell receives an envelope from the owner in a back room.

scenes.heading: INT. BUFALINO’S HOUSE - NIGHT - 1960’S
scenes.summary: Russell cooks Christmas dinner, slicing prosciutto and stirring it into sauce while Frank questions his cooking habits.

scenes.heading: INT. BUFALINO’S HOUSE - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Russell and Carrie eat Christmas dinner with Frank, Irene, and the girls.

scenes.heading: INT. BUFALINO’S HOUSE - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The families open Christmas gifts around the tree while Peggy remains uncomfortable in Russell's presence.

scenes.heading: INT. BUFALINO’S HOUSE - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank and Russell drink limoncello privately in the den and discuss how phone surveillance has made communication impossible.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - DAY - FLASHBACK
scenes.summary: Frank and Russell have a confusing phone conversation about 'friends' before agreeing to meet in person.

scenes.heading: EXT. PHILLY - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank drives to a South Philly trucking company and speaks to a man about getting a rig.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank drives a Milestone semi truck down the highway toward Baltimore.

scenes.heading: EXT. CONCRETE PLANT - BALTIMORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank meets Dave Ferrie at a concrete plant with a landing strip and watches Maryland National Guardsmen load weapons into his truck.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank drives the weapons-loaded truck down Route 13 toward Florida at night.

scenes.heading: EXT. JACKSONVILLE, FLORIDA - DAWN
scenes.summary: Frank delivers the truck to a man with surgically-altered ears at a Jacksonville dog track and watches Cubans unload the weapons.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank drives the replacement car back north.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank and his family watch TV coverage of the failed Bay of Pigs invasion while Castro gives an anti-American speech.

scenes.heading: EXT. MIAMI - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank observes the sun-bleached Miami Beach coastline in 1961.

scenes.heading: EXT. DEAUVILLE HOTEL - MIAMI - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank arrives by cab at the Deauville Hotel for the 1961 International Teamsters convention.

scenes.heading: INT. DEAUVILLE HOTEL - MIAMI - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank stands at check-in tables scrutinizing union members' faces as they show their cards for convention admittance.

scenes.heading: INT. DEAUVILLE HOTEL BALLROOM - MIAMI - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank surveys convention delegates for weapons or cameras while Jimmy addresses them and introduces Frank Fitzsimmons as the new vice president.

scenes.heading: INT. DEAUVILLE HOTEL SUITE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jimmy pours Frank ginger ale and expresses concern about Joe McGreal's shake-down activities in Philadelphia Local 107.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRUCKING COMPANY - PHILADELPHIA - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank parks his Cadillac at the same trucking company McGreal had shaken down.

scenes.heading: INT. TRUCKING COMPANY OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank introduces himself to the trucking company owner and refuses the envelope, shocking the man who expected another shakedown.
scenes.turningPointNote: Frank establishes his legitimate leadership style, ending McGreal's corruption

scenes.heading: INT. LOCAL 107 OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank works at his desk in the Local 107 office conducting legitimate union business with other union members.

scenes.heading: INT. COURTROOM - NASHVILLE - DAY
scenes.summary: A young man in a raincoat emerges from courthouse crowds and walks toward Jimmy's defense table during a trial recess.

scenes.heading: INT. COURTHOUSE - NASHVILLE - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank walks alongside Jimmy as his bodyguard toward the Nashville courtroom.

scenes.heading: INT. ANDREW JACKSON HOTEL - NASHVILLE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank watches the Cuban Missile Crisis on TV with his gun on the coffee table while Jimmy strategizes with attorneys eating fried chicken and Ed Partin guards the door.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY - TENNESSEE - DAY
scenes.summary: A man gets out of a parked Lincoln on a deserted Tennessee highway and hands an envelope to a state trooper.

scenes.heading: INT. ANDREW JACKSON HOTEL SUITE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jimmy and his lawyers arrange surveillance photos of jurors on a coffee table, adding a checkmark to one labeled 'Patrolman's Wife' while Frank watches TV and Ed Partin guards the door.

scenes.heading: INT. A BATHROOM SOMEWHERE - DAY
scenes.summary: Ed Partin removes a small tape recorder that was taped to his ribs.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Ed Partin places the tape recorder on federal prosecutor Walter Sheridan's coffee table.

scenes.heading: INT. COURTROOM - NASHVILLE - DAY
scenes.summary: A Teamster witness takes the Fifth Amendment in Jimmy's Nashville trial after Jimmy signals him from the defense table.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCAL 107 - PHILADELPHIA - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank arrives at Local 107 to find everyone gathered around a television watching news of President Kennedy's assassination.
scenes.turningPointNote: Kennedy's assassination fundamentally shifts the political landscape and organized crime prosecutions

scenes.heading: EXT. WASHINGTON DC - DAY
scenes.summary: Jimmy emerges from Teamster headquarters and tells reporters that Bobby Kennedy is 'just another lawyer now' following the President's death.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA D’ROMA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Russell sits at his usual table at Villa D'Roma dipping bread in wine with his wife.

scenes.heading: INT. COURTROOM - NASHVILLE - DAY
scenes.summary: Jimmy stands before the judge for sentencing after being convicted of jury tampering while Frank watches from the courtroom.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY - DAY - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank and Russell change a flat tire on their Cadillac while their wives smoke cigarettes.

scenes.heading: EXT. PENNSYLVANIA - DAY - 1960’S
scenes.summary: A prison bus drives past on the same highway.

scenes.heading: EXT. LEWISBURG FEDERAL PENITENTIARY - DAY
scenes.summary: The prison bus pulls through the gates of Lewisburg Federal Penitentiary.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jimmy's imprisonment removes him from power and sets up the conflicts that will define the rest of the story

scenes.heading: INT. LEWISBURG PENITENTIARY - DAY
scenes.summary: Jimmy Hoffa is processed into Lewisburg Penitentiary, fingerprinted, photographed, and given a prison uniform.

scenes.heading: INT. LEWISBURG PENITENTIARY - DAY
scenes.summary: Jimmy eats spaghetti, meatballs, and ice cream in the prison cafeteria with other Teamsters and mobsters.

scenes.heading: INT. VISITORS ROOM - LEWISBURG - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank Fitzsimmons sits down with Jimmy and his lawyers in the prison visitors room to discuss running the Teamsters.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOLF COURSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Fitzsimmons hits a golf ball off a tee.

scenes.heading: INT. LEWISBURG PENITENTIARY - DAY
scenes.summary: Tony Provenzano moves through the prison cafeteria line alongside taller inmates.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW JERSEY - DAY
scenes.summary: A car drives down a New Jersey highway past farms.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Sally Bugs strangles a man with a nylon rope from the back seat of a moving car.

scenes.heading: INT. LEWISBURG PENITENTIARY - CONTINUED - DAY
scenes.summary: Tony Pro confronts Jimmy about his lost pension while Jimmy eats ice cream, with Jimmy explaining that Pro forfeited his pension for extortion while Jimmy's remains intact.
scenes.turningPointNote: The pension dispute between Jimmy and Tony Pro establishes the personal animosity that will later prove fatal

scenes.heading: INT. SHOE STORE - OHIO - DAY - 1975
scenes.summary: Irene and Carrie try on shoes while Russell collects an envelope from the store owner and Frank looks at spiked golf shoes.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOLF COURSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Fitzsimmons lines up a putt on a golf course.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEN DORFMAN’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Allen Dorfman's Lincoln Continental is riddled with shotgun blasts as he backs out of his garage, with Dorfman reaching for his gun while the car is hit repeatedly.

scenes.heading: EXT. WRECKING YARD - DAY
scenes.summary: Fitz nervously regards Dorfman's destroyed Lincoln at a wrecking yard while Dorfman stands unharmed, and Robert Kennedy announces his presidential run on television.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA D’ROMA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank, Russell, Angelo, Skinny and other regulars celebrate at Villa D'Roma's bar, toasting new Attorney General Ramsey Clark.

scenes.heading: INT. LEWISBURG PENITENTIARY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jimmy paces in his prison cell at night.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Kitchen staff work chaotically preparing food for hundreds of people in a Miami hotel kitchen.

scenes.heading: INT. BALLROOM - MIAMI HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank observes hotel staff setting up a ballroom with a large picture of Fitz on stage while Jimmy's picture is relegated to the lobby corner.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY - MIAMI HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank has hotel maintenance workers rescue Jimmy's poster from the lobby corner.

scenes.heading: INT. BALLROOM - MIAMI HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Jimmy's wife Jo speaks to the Teamster convention with both Jimmy's and Fitz's posters flanking the stage, while Frank notices Fitz's lukewarm applause.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA D’ROMA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Russell asks Frank to help with another matter after Frank reports that Jimmy wants to get out of prison.

scenes.heading: EXT. COLUMBUS CIRCLE - DAY
scenes.summary: A black man shoots Joe Colombo at an Italian-American Civil Rights League rally in front of Colombo's family, then is shot by Colombo's bodyguards.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Crazy Joey Gallo climbs out of a car with his wife and bodyguard, smiling and waving to photographers before entering a nightclub.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA D’ROMA - CONTINUED
scenes.summary: Russell tells Frank that Joey Gallo is shaking down restaurants in Little Italy.

scenes.heading: INT. NIGHTCLUB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Crazy Joey shares a table with glamorous types at a nightclub.

scenes.heading: INT. MOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank examines a pile of guns on a motel room coffee table, selecting appropriate weapons while rejecting others based on stopping power and noise considerations.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK - DAY
scenes.summary: Downtown guys sit outside a social club in Little Italy while tourists take pictures on the corner during a normal spring day.

scenes.heading: INT. UMBERTO’S CLAM HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: The interior of Umberto's Clam House is surveyed, noting the two entrances and table arrangements, while a waiter passes with spaghetti and clam sauce.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank's teenage daughter Peggy secretly watches through a bathroom door crack as her father puts a .32 and .38 in his waistband and puts on his jacket.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Peggy appears at the top of the stairs as Frank is leaving late at night, asking where he's going.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. FRANK’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank gets into John Francis's car while Peggy watches from a window.

scenes.heading: EXT. LITTLE ITALY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Crazy Joey's Lincoln pulls up to Umberto's Clam House and he helps his wife and others out, while Frank gets out of John Francis's car on Mott Street.

scenes.heading: INT. UMBERTO’S CLAM HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Frank enters Umberto's Clam House, shoots Gallo's bodyguard, then shoots Gallo once inside and twice more on the sidewalk before getting into John Francis's car.
scenes.turningPointNote: Frank commits a high-profile assassination that demonstrates his lethal capabilities and deepens his involvement in mob violence

scenes.heading: EXT. YONKERS - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank throws the gun into the Hudson River while John Francis waits in the car.

scenes.heading: INT. COFFEE SHOP - OHIO - DAY - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank washes his hands in a coffee shop restroom, then notices Russell possibly stopping his phone conversation when Frank passes by.

scenes.heading: INT. PAROLE HEARING ROOM - DAY - 1973
scenes.summary: Jimmy sits before a parole board reading from a prepared statement about devoting his life to education.

scenes.heading: INT. LEWISBURG PENITENTIARY - DAY
scenes.summary: Jimmy is escorted down the prison cell block by guards.

scenes.heading: EXT. LEWISBURG PENITENTIARY - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank and Jimmy's lawyers escort Jimmy past reporters and photographers as he's released from Lewisburg Penitentiary.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jimmy's release from prison sets up the final act conflicts over Teamster leadership and his relationship with the mob

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMS - MIAMI - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank waits at a Lums hotdog stand pickup counter while Watergate hearings play on television, ordering chili dogs steamed in beer.

scenes.heading: INT. JIMMY’S CONDO - MIAMI - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank and Jimmy eat chili dogs in Jimmy's Miami condo while Jimmy complains about Fitz thinking he runs the Teamsters and refusing to step down.

scenes.heading: INT. PRIVATE CLUB - MIAMI - DAY
scenes.summary: Jimmy and Tony Pro have a tense confrontation at a Miami private club where Pro arrives late in casual attire, leading to an escalating argument about respect and their pension dispute.
scenes.turningPointNote: The heated confrontation between Jimmy and Tony Pro escalates their conflict to a dangerous level, setting up the final tragedy

scenes.heading: INT. CAR - MOVING - MIAMI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank drives Jimmy back to his condo in silence after the disastrous meeting, with Jimmy asking if Russell would do something about Tony Pro.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank and Russell arrive at Vesuvius restaurant, with Frank hoping in voiceover that calmer heads might prevail in resolving Jimmy's conflict.

scenes.heading: INT. VESUVIUS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Salerno tells Frank and Russell he disapproves of Pro's comments about Jimmy's grandchildren but won't control what Pro says, while acknowledging someone needs to calm Jimmy down.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOLF COURSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Fitz plays golf with Tony Pro while Frank notes in voiceover that Jimmy dealt with Fitz instead of Pro for a while.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEAMSTER OFFICES - DC - DAY
scenes.summary: Jimmy gives a televised interview outside his former office, publicly criticizing Fitz for traveling to golf tournaments while collecting a full-time Teamster salary.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARINA - LAKE MICHIGAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Dave Johnson and his family approach their boat at a marina when it suddenly explodes, as Frank explains this was Fitz's response to Jimmy's criticism.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - DETROIT - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard Fitzsimmons walks toward his Lincoln Continental outside the Nemo Bar when it explodes, as Frank explains this was Jimmy's message back to Fitz.

scenes.heading: EXT. UNION HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Jo Hoffa loads files into her car trunk and nervously starts the ignition after being fired from her union job, but the car doesn't explode.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Jimmy appears on Meet The Press, publicly accusing Frank Fitzsimmons of selling out the union to organized crime and giving mob-controlled pension fund loans.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jimmy crosses the line by publicly accusing Fitzsimmons of mob connections, escalating the conflict to a dangerous level

scenes.heading: INT. VESUVIUS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Salerno questions Russell and Frank about whether Jimmy is serious about his organized crime accusations, expressing concern that either way it's problematic.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank walks with Jimmy and his dog, delivering Salerno's message that Jimmy should retire, but Jimmy refuses and asks Frank to relay his defiance back.

scenes.heading: INT. THE LATIN CASINO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank's appreciation night begins at the Latin Casino with mob bosses, politicians, and Frank's family in attendance.

scenes.heading: INT. THE LATIN CASINO - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Russell privately confronts Jimmy about his behavior, suggesting he's demonstrating a failure to show appreciation, but Jimmy insists it's his union.

scenes.heading: INT. THE LATIN CASINO - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jimmy speaks at the podium praising Frank's union service and arrest record while Russell and Salerno watch from their table.

scenes.heading: INT. THE LATIN CASINO - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jimmy and Frank pose for photographs together, with Jimmy expressing genuine appreciation for Frank's support and Frank calling it an honor.

scenes.heading: INT. THE LATIN CASINO - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Russell gives Frank a special gold ring with an Italian coin, telling him only three people in the world have one and only one is Irish.
scenes.turningPointNote: Russell formally inducts Frank into the inner circle with the special ring, deepening his commitment to the organization

scenes.heading: INT. THE LATIN CASINO - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank warns Jimmy in the men's room that Salerno is serious about his threats, but Jimmy remains defiant and claims to have insurance records ready for the press.

scenes.heading: INT. CADILLAC - MOVING - DAY - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank drives Russell and their wives toward Detroit in 1975, with Frank explaining in voiceover that their real purpose is a peace mission.

scenes.heading: EXT. GAS STATION - OHIO - DAY - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank calls Jimmy from a gas station phone booth, arranging a meeting to work things out, with Jimmy initially refusing to attend the wedding.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD JOHNSONS - OHIO - DAY
scenes.summary: The group checks into a Howard Johnson's motel in Ohio.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD JOHNSONS - OHIO - LATER - DAY
scenes.summary: Russell suggests Frank call Jimmy again to see if he's reconsidered the meeting.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD JOHNSONS - OHIO - LATER - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank calls Jimmy from the motel, learning that Jimmy has changed his mind and will meet with Tony Pro at the Red Fox restaurant, arranged by Tony Jack.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jimmy agrees to the meeting that will lead to his death, setting the final act in motion

scenes.heading: INT. MOTEL ROOM - HOWARD JOHNSONS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Frank and Irene change clothes for dinner while Frank speculates in voiceover about Jimmy's possible motives for agreeing to meet Pro.

scenes.heading: INT. ITALIAN RESTAURANT - OHIO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The two couples dine at Russell's Italian restaurant, with Frank describing the meal in detail in voiceover.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT KITCHEN - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Russell prepares his special salad dressing in the kitchen while informing Frank of a change in plans to drive up in the afternoon instead of morning.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD JOHNSONS MOTEL ROOM OHIO - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank lies awake in bed next to his sleeping wife with the phone on the nightstand beside him.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD JOHNSONS - OHIO - MORNING
scenes.summary: Russell tells Frank over breakfast that they're going to Port Clinton while the women stay at the motel, and Frank lets his cereal get soggy.

scenes.heading: INT. CADILLAC - MOVING - HIGHWAY 80 - OHIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank drives Russell toward Port Clinton while reflecting in voiceover that he had to participate to protect himself and Irene since he knew too much.

scenes.heading: EXT. AIRSTRIP - PORT CLINTON - OHIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank boards a small plane at the Port Clinton airstrip while Russell remains in the car, with Frank explaining the alibi in voiceover.

scenes.heading: EXT. MICHIGAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank's plane lands at Pontiac Airfield and he gets into a waiting Ford with keys under the mat.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. FORD - PONTIAC, MICHIGAN - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank drives past the Red Fox restaurant on Telegraph Road, noting the gun in the glove compartment and checking his watch before turning onto Seven Mile Road.

scenes.heading: EXT. PONTIAC, MICHIGAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank locates the designated house with brown shingles and notes a Buick in the driveway while reflecting on what will happen to Jimmy's body.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE - PONTIAC - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Frank enters the house where Sally Bugs is cutting linoleum and two young Italians play cards in the kitchen, with Sally noting that Chuckie is late.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSE - PONTIAC - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Frank and Sally Bugs approach Chuckie's Mercury outside the house, with Frank feeling sorry for Chuckie who doesn't know he's part of the setup.

scenes.heading: INT. MERCURY - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Chuckie drives Frank and Sally Bugs toward the Red Fox while Sally Bugs questions him about fish, and Frank checks his watch showing 2:40.

scenes.heading: EXT. RED FOX RESTAURANT - DAY
scenes.summary: Jimmy angrily confronts the late arrivals at the Red Fox parking lot, cursing Chuckie and questioning Sally Bugs before getting into the car when he sees Frank.

scenes.heading: INT. CHUCKIE’S MERCURY - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Jimmy berates Frank for not informing him earlier about the change in meeting location while they drive toward the house.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSE - PONTIAC - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jimmy and Frank get out at the house while Sally Bugs takes the passenger seat as Chuckie drives away, with Jimmy asking Frank if he has his gun.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE - PONTIAC - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jimmy immediately senses danger upon entering the quiet house with no voices and poorly cut linoleum underfoot, while Frank confirms in voiceover that Jimmy knew what it was.
scenes.turningPointNote: The moment of Jimmy Hoffa's murder - he realizes the trap as he enters the house

scenes.heading: EXT. PORT CLINTON - LATER - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank returns to the Port Clinton airstrip where Russell wakes from his nap in the Cadillac and can tell the Detroit errand has been completed.

scenes.heading: INT. CHURCH - DETROIT - NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: Frank attends the wedding at a Detroit church, wiping tears from his eyes as he looks at the ring Russell gave him and the watch Jimmy gave him.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR - MOVING - NIGHT - 1999
scenes.summary: An older Frank in 1999 passes the same Howard Johnson's motel with the same expression of grief and remorse on his face.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - PHILADELPHIA - DAY - 1975
scenes.summary: Frank returns home to find his family watching TV news about Hoffa's disappearance, with Peggy studying her father suspiciously as he says he should call Jo.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank calls the crying Jo Hoffa from his bedroom, holding the phone receiver to his head like a gun before telling her everything will be all right.

scenes.heading: EXT. FUNERAL PARLOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Two Italians carry a body in a black garbage bag from the Buick trunk to the back door of a funeral parlor.

scenes.heading: INT. FUNERAL PARLOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A pine coffin burns in a cremation oven, with Frank explaining in voiceover how the intense heat melts everything but leaves a body shape like Pompeii.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAND JURY ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank repeatedly takes the Fifth Amendment during grand jury questioning, even when asked about the color of the prosecutor's pen.

scenes.heading: INT. COURTHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank and his lawyer exit the courthouse as Frank explains in voiceover that everyone was convicted of other crimes but no one talked about the murder.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE HOUSE IN PONTIAC - DAY - FLASHBACK
scenes.summary: Frank explains in voiceover that the Andretta brothers got twenty years for extorting a trucking company.

scenes.heading: INT. PRISON - DAY
scenes.summary: Tony Pro walks through a prison cell block as Frank explains he was already serving time for another conviction.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR - MOVING - DAY - FLASHBACK
scenes.summary: A flashback shows Sally Bugs strangling the Secretary-Treasurer with a nylon rope in a car, which Pro was finally convicted for.

scenes.heading: EXT. FEDERAL BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: Someone watches Sally Bugs enter a federal building from far away, with Frank's voiceover explaining that Sally didn't tell anyone about the visit, which suggests he's cooperating with authorities.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank and John Francis approach Sally Bugs outside the Andrea Doria Social Club, and Frank shoots Sally twice in the head while he waits for an introduction, with John Francis firing three additional shots.

scenes.heading: INT. OPERATING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Tony Salerno undergoes a colonoscopy on a surgical table while Frank's voiceover explains he was arrested for income tax charges and diagnosed with cancer the same week.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA D’ROMA - DAY
scenes.summary: Russell has a cordial conversation with Jack Napoli at Villa D'Roma while Frank's voiceover reveals Russell was caught on tape threatening Napoli over unpaid jewelry debt.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLA D’ROMA - DAY
scenes.summary: Federal agents escort Russell out of Villa D'Roma while Frank's voiceover explains that Napoli was wired and the conversation was recorded as evidence of extortion.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR WASH - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank sits in his car going through a car wash while his voiceover explains he was prosecuted for buying a Cadillac at under-market value from Eugene Boffa, which authorities claimed was a bribe.

scenes.heading: EXT. SANDSTONE PRISON - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank's voiceover describes Sandstone Prison in Minnesota as a remote facility near the Canadian border that's much colder than other cities.

scenes.heading: EXT. SANDSTONE PRISON - DAY
scenes.summary: Russell, Salerno and other elderly inmates in wheelchairs play bocce in the snow while Frank watches, with his voiceover detailing their various health deteriorations including Russell's Parkinson's and Frank's neuropathy.

scenes.heading: INT. SANDSTONE CAFETERIA - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank brings prosciutto bread to Russell in the prison cafeteria, where Russell expresses regret about Jimmy Hoffa's fate and apologizes for not protecting Frank better.

scenes.heading: EXT. SANDSTONE PRISON - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank watches Russell being wheeled away to church, with his voiceover revealing that Russell went from church to the prison hospital to the graveyard.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAVEYARD - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank attends Russell's sparsely attended funeral, then stands at his wife Irene's grave with three daughters while Peggy stands apart and refuses to look at him.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank moves through his house on aluminum canes, emptying ashtrays filled with lipstick-stained cigarette butts.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Frank sits alone in a chair drinking in front of a television he's not watching.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Frank attempts to organize dozens of pills in a weekly container but gets confused and starts over.

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank trips and falls in a dark hallway while walking with his canes in pajamas and cannot get back up.

scenes.heading: INT. ASSISTED LIVING FACILITY - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank sits apart from other residents in his wheelchair at an assisted living facility rec room, wearing aviator sunglasses and his gold jewelry.
scenes.turningPointNote: Frank's transition to assisted living marks his final decline into complete isolation

scenes.heading: INT. ASSISTED LIVING FACILITY - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Frank shows a nurse a photograph of Peggy and Jimmy Hoffa at miniature golf, explaining that Peggy is his daughter who hasn't visited, and reveals he has four daughters who rarely come around.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASSISTED LIVING FACILITY - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Two young FBI agents visit Frank in the courtyard, informing him that his attorney Ragano died of cancer and that everyone from his era is dead, while asking him to help Hoffa's surviving children find closure.

scenes.heading: EXT. BANK - OUTSIDE PHILLY - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank struggles out of a taxi with his canes outside a bank, refusing help from an orderly.

scenes.heading: INT. BANK - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank waits in line at a bank to approach his daughter Peggy's teller window, but she puts up her closed sign and walks away as he pleads that he's dying and just wants to talk.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSE - PHILLY - DAY
scenes.summary: A taxi sits parked outside a small house in Philadelphia.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE - PHILLY - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank asks his daughter Delores to call Peggy so he can apologize, but Delores confronts him about how his violent solutions to their problems made them afraid to come to him with any issues.

scenes.heading: INT. CASKET STORE - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Frank shops for his own casket at a warehouse-style store, telling the salesman he wants burial rather than cremation and that the casket is for himself.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank sits in a hospital room hooked to IVs, being interviewed on video about his confession to a monsignor, explaining he told the priest he'd done things he wasn't proud of but didn't need to provide details for absolution.
scenes.turningPointNote: Frank's final interview reveals his attempt at spiritual reconciliation while maintaining his secrets to the end

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
